name: pyensembl

metadata.name: pyensembl
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Python interface to Ensembl reference genome metadata

documentation:
[![Tests](https://github.com/openvax/pyensembl/actions/workflows/tests.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/openvax/pyensembl/actions/workflows/tests.yml) [![Coverage Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/openvax/pyensembl/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://coveralls.io/github/openvax/pyensembl?branch=main) # PyEnsembl PyEnsembl is a Python interface to [Ensembl](http://www.ensembl.org) reference genome metadata such as exons and transcripts. PyEnsembl downloads [GTF](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gene_transfer_format) and [FASTA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/FASTA_format) files from the [Ensembl FTP server](ftp://ftp.ensembl.org) and loads them into a local database. PyEnsembl can also work with custom reference data specified using user-supplied GTF and FASTA files. # Example Usage ```python from pyensembl import EnsemblRelease # release 77 uses human reference genome GRCh38 data = EnsemblRelease(77) # will return ['HLA-A'] gene_names = data.gene_names_at_locus(contig=6, position=29945884) # get all exons associated with HLA-A exon_ids = data.exon_ids_of_gene_name('HLA-A') ``` # Installation You can install PyEnsembl using [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/latest/quickstart.html): ```sh pip install pyensembl ``` This should also install any required packages such as [datacache](https://github.com/openvax/datacache). Before using PyEnsembl, run the following command to download and install Ensembl data: ``` pyensembl install --release --species ``` For example, `pyensembl install --release 75 76 --species human` will download and install all human reference data from Ensembl releases 75 and 76. Alternatively, you can create the `EnsemblRelease` object from inside a Python process and call `ensembl_object.download()` followed by `ensembl_object.index()`. ## Cache Location By default, PyEnsembl uses the platform-specific `Cache` folder and caches the files into the `pyensembl` sub-directory. You can override this default by setting the environment key `PYENSEMBL_CACHE_DIR` as your preferred location for caching: ```sh export PYENSEMBL_CACHE_DIR=/custom/cache/dir ``` or ```python import os os.environ['PYENSEMBL_CACHE_DIR'] = '/custom/cache/dir' # ... PyEnsembl API usage ``` # Usage tips ## List installed genomes To see the genomes for which PyEnsembl has already downloaded and indexed metadata you can run: ```sh pyensembl list ``` Or equivalently do this in Python: ```python from pyensembl.shell import collect_all_installed_ensembl_releases collect_all_installed_ensembl_releases() ``` ## Load genome in Python Here's an example Python snippet that loads fly genome data from Ensembl release v100: ```python from pyensembl import EnsemblRelease data = EnsemblRelease(release=100, species='drosophila_melanogaster') ``` ## Data structures ### Gene ```python gene = genome.gene_by_id(gene_id='FBgn0011747') ``` ### Transcript ```python transcript = gene.transcripts[0] ``` ### Protein information ```python transcript.protein_id transcript.protein_sequence ``` # Non-Ensembl Data PyEnsembl also allows arbitrary genomes via the specification of local file paths or remote URLs to both Ensembl and non-Ensembl GTF and FASTA files. (Warning: GTF formats can vary, and handling of non-Ensembl data is still very much in development.) For example: ```python from pyensembl import Genome data = Genome( reference_name='GRCh38', annotation_name='my_genome_features', # annotation_version=None, gtf_path_or_url='/My/local/gtf/path_to_my_genome_features.gtf', # Path or URL of GTF file # transcript_fasta_paths_or_urls=None, # List of paths or URLs of FASTA files containing transcript sequences # protein_fasta_paths_or_urls=None, # List of paths or URLs of FASTA files containing protein sequences # cache_directory_path=None, # Where to place downloaded and cached files for this genome ) # parse GTF and construct database of genomic features data.index() gene_names = data.gene_names_at_locus(contig=6, position=29945884) ``` # API The `EnsemblRelease` object has methods to let you access all possible combinations of the annotation features _gene_name_, _gene_id_, _transcript_name_, _transcript_id_, _exon_id_ as well as the location of these genomic elements (contig, start position, end position, strand). ## Genes
genes(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns a list of Gene objects, optionally restricted to a particular contig or strand.
genes_at_locus(contig, position, end=None, strand=None)
Returns a list of Gene objects overlapping a particular position on a contig, optionally extend into a range with the end parameter and restrict to forward or backward strand by passing strand='+' or strand='-'.
gene_by_id(gene_id)
Return a Gene object for given Ensembl gene ID (e.g. "ENSG00000068793").
gene_names(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns all gene names in the annotation database, optionally restricted to a particular contig or strand.
genes_by_name(gene_name)
Get all the unqiue genes with the given name (there might be multiple due to copies in the genome), return a list containing a Gene object for each distinct ID.
gene_by_protein_id(protein_id)
Find Gene associated with the given Ensembl protein ID (e.g. "ENSP00000350283")
gene_names_at_locus(contig, position, end=None, strand=None)
Names of genes overlapping with the given locus, optionally restricted by strand. (returns a list to account for overlapping genes)
gene_name_of_gene_id(gene_id)
Returns name of gene with given genen ID.
gene_name_of_transcript_id(transcript_id)
Returns name of gene associated with given transcript ID.
gene_name_of_transcript_name(transcript_name)
Returns name of gene associated with given transcript name.
gene_name_of_exon_id(exon_id)
Returns name of gene associated with given exon ID.
gene_ids(contig=None, strand=None)
Return all gene IDs in the annotation database, optionally restricted by chromosome name or strand.
gene_ids_of_gene_name(gene_name)
Returns all Ensembl gene IDs with the given name.
## Transcripts
transcripts(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns a list of Transcript objects for all transcript entries in the Ensembl database, optionally restricted to a particular contig or strand.
transcript_by_id(transcript_id)
Construct a Transcript object for given Ensembl transcript ID (e.g. "ENST00000369985")
transcripts_by_name(transcript_name)
Returns a list of Transcript objects for every transcript matching the given name.
transcript_names(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns all transcript names in the annotation database.
transcript_ids(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns all transcript IDs in the annotation database.
transcript_ids_of_gene_id(gene_id)
Return IDs of all transcripts associated with given gene ID.
transcript_ids_of_gene_name(gene_name)
Return IDs of all transcripts associated with given gene name.
transcript_ids_of_transcript_name(transcript_name)
Find all Ensembl transcript IDs with the given name.
transcript_ids_of_exon_id(exon_id)
Return IDs of all transcripts associatd with given exon ID.
## Exons
exon_ids(contig=None, strand=None)
Returns a list of exons IDs in the annotation database, optionally restricted by the given chromosome and strand.
exon_by_id(exon_id)
Construct an Exon object for given Ensembl exon ID (e.g. "ENSE00001209410")
exon_ids_of_gene_id(gene_id)
Returns a list of exon IDs associated with a given gene ID.
exon_ids_of_gene_name(gene_name)
Returns a list of exon IDs associated with a given gene name.
exon_ids_of_transcript_id(transcript_id)
Returns a list of exon IDs associated with a given transcript ID.
exon_ids_of_transcript_name(transcript_name)
Returns a list of exon IDs associated with a given transcript name.

classes.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.id
documentation: Alias for transcript_id necessary for backward compatibility.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.name
documentation: Alias for transcript_name necessary for backward compatibility.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.__len__
documentation: Length of a transcript is the sum of its exon lengths

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript._transcript_feature_position_ranges
documentation:
Find start/end chromosomal position range of features
(such as start codon) for this transcript.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript._transcript_feature_positions
documentation: Get unique positions for feature, raise an error if feature is absent.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript._codon_positions
documentation:
Parameters
----------
feature : str
    Possible values are "start_codon" or "stop_codon"

Returns list of three chromosomal positions.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.contains_start_codon
documentation: Does this transcript have an annotated start_codon entry?

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.contains_stop_codon
documentation: Does this transcript have an annotated stop_codon entry?

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.start_codon_complete
documentation: Does the start codon span 3 genomic positions?

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.start_codon_positions
documentation: Chromosomal positions of nucleotides in start codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.stop_codon_positions
documentation: Chromosomal positions of nucleotides in stop codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.exon_intervals
documentation:
List of (start,end) tuples for each exon of this transcript,
in the order specified by the 'exon_number' column of the
exon table.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.spliced_offset
documentation:
Convert from an absolute chromosomal position to the offset into
this transcript"s spliced mRNA.

Position must be inside some exon (otherwise raise exception).

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.start_codon_unspliced_offsets
documentation:
Offsets from start of unspliced pre-mRNA transcript
of nucleotides in start codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.stop_codon_unspliced_offsets
documentation:
Offsets from start of unspliced pre-mRNA transcript
of nucleotides in stop codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript._contiguous_offsets
documentation:
Sorts the input list of integer offsets,
ensures that values are contiguous.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.start_codon_spliced_offsets
documentation:
Offsets from start of spliced mRNA transcript
of nucleotides in start codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.stop_codon_spliced_offsets
documentation:
Offsets from start of spliced mRNA transcript
of nucleotides in stop codon.

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.coding_sequence_position_ranges
documentation:
Return absolute chromosome position ranges for CDS fragments
of this transcript

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.complete
documentation:
Consider a transcript complete if it has start and stop codons and
a coding sequence whose length is divisible by 3

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.sequence
documentation:
Spliced cDNA sequence of transcript
(includes 5" UTR, coding sequence, and 3" UTR)

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.first_start_codon_spliced_offset
documentation:
Offset of first nucleotide in start codon into the spliced mRNA
(excluding introns)

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.last_stop_codon_spliced_offset
documentation:
Offset of last nucleotide in stop codon into the spliced mRNA
(excluding introns)

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.coding_sequence
documentation:
cDNA coding sequence (from start codon to stop codon, without
any introns)

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.five_prime_utr_sequence
documentation:
cDNA sequence of 5' UTR
(untranslated region at the beginning of the transcript)

methods.name: pyensembl.transcript.Transcript.three_prime_utr_sequence
documentation:
cDNA sequence of 3' UTR
(untranslated region at the end of the transcript)

Transcript encompasses the locus, exons, and sequence of a transcript.

Lazily fetches sequence in case we"re constructing many Transcripts
and not using the sequence, avoid the memory/performance overhead
of fetching and storing sequences from a FASTA file.

classes.name: pyensembl.locus_with_genome.LocusWithGenome

methods.name: pyensembl.locus_with_genome.LocusWithGenome.is_protein_coding
documentation:
We're not counting immunoglobulin-like genes from the T-cell receptor or
or antibodies since they occur in fragments that must be recombined.
It might be worth consider counting non-sense mediated decay and
non-stop decay since variants in these could potentially make a
functional protein. To read more about the biotypes used in Ensembl:
    http://vega.sanger.ac.uk/info/about/gene_and_transcript_types.html
    http://www.gencodegenes.org/gencode_biotypes.html

For now let's stick with the simple category of 'protein_coding', which
means that there is an open reading frame in this gene/transcript
whose successful transcription has been observed.

Common base class for Gene and Transcript to avoid copying
their shared logic.

functions.name: pyensembl.search.find_nearest_locus
documentation:
Finds nearest locus (object with method `distance_to_interval`) to the
interval defined by the given `start` and `end` positions.
Returns the distance to that locus, along with the locus object itself.

modules.name: pyensembl.genome
documentation:
Contains the Genome class, with its millions of accessors and wrappers
around an arbitrary genomic database.

classes.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
reference_name : str
    Name of genome assembly which annotations in GTF are aligned against
    (and from which sequence data is drawn)

annotation_name : str
    Name of annotation source (e.g. "Ensembl)

annotation_version : int or str
    Version of annotation database (e.g. 75)

gtf_path_or_url : str
    Path or URL of GTF file

transcript_fasta_paths_or_urls : list
    List of paths or URLs of FASTA files containing transcript sequences

protein_fasta_paths_or_urls : list
    List of paths or URLs of FASTA files containing protein sequences

decompress_on_download : bool
    If remote file is compressed, decompress the local copy?

copy_local_files_to_cache : bool
    If genome data file is local use it directly or copy to cache first?

cache_directory_path : None
    Where to place downloaded and cached files for this genome,
    by default inferred from reference name, annotation name,
    annotation version, and global cache dir for pyensembl.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.to_dict
documentation: Returns a dictionary of the essential fields of this Genome.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._init_lazy_fields
documentation: Member data that gets loaded or constructed on demand

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._get_cached_path
documentation:
Get the local path for a possibly remote file, invoking either
a download or install error message if it's missing.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.download
documentation:
Download data files needed by this Genome instance.

Parameters
----------
overwrite : bool, optional
    Download files regardless whether local copy already exists.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.index
documentation:
Assuming that all necessary data for this Genome has been downloaded,
generate the GTF database and save efficient representation of
FASTA sequence files.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.install_string
documentation:
Add every missing file to the install string shown to the user
in an error message.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.clear_cache
documentation: Clear any in-memory cached values

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.delete_index_files
documentation: Delete all data aside from source GTF and FASTA files

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._all_feature_values
documentation:
Cached lookup of all values for a particular feature property from
the database, caches repeated queries in memory and
stores them as a CSV.

Parameters
----------

column : str
    Name of property (e.g. exon_id)

feature : str
    Type of entry (e.g. exon)

distinct : bool, optional
    Keep only unique values

contig : str, optional
    Restrict query to particular contig

strand : str, optional
    Restrict results to "+" or "-" strands

Returns a list constructed from query results.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.transcript_sequence
documentation:
Return cDNA nucleotide sequence of transcript, or None if
transcript doesn't have cDNA sequence.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.protein_sequence
documentation:
Return cDNA nucleotide sequence of transcript, or None if
transcript doesn't have cDNA sequence.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.locus_of_gene_id
documentation: Given a gene ID returns Locus with: chromosome, start, stop, strand
Methods which return Locus objects
containing (contig, start, stop, strand)
of various genomic entities

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.loci_of_gene_names
documentation:
Given a gene name returns list of Locus objects with fields:
    chromosome, start, stop, strand
You can get multiple results since a gene might have multiple copies
in the genome.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.locus_of_exon_id
documentation: Given an exon ID returns Locus

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.contigs
documentation:
Returns all contig names for any gene in the genome
(field called "seqname" in Ensembl GTF files)
Contigs

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.genes
documentation:
Returns all Gene objects in the database. Can be restricted to a
particular contig/chromosome and strand by the following arguments:

Parameters
----------
contig : str
    Only return genes on the given contig.

strand : str
    Only return genes on this strand.
Gene Info Objects

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_by_id
documentation: Construct a Gene object for the given gene ID.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.genes_by_name
documentation:
Get all the unqiue genes with the given name (there might be multiple
due to copies in the genome), return a list containing a Gene object
for each distinct ID.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_by_protein_id
documentation:
Get the gene ID associated with the given protein ID,
return its Gene object

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._query_gene_name
documentation: Gene Names

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_names
documentation:
Return all genes in the database,
optionally restrict to a chromosome and/or strand.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._query_gene_ids
documentation: Gene IDs

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_ids
documentation:
What are all the gene IDs
(optionally restrict to a given chromosome/contig and/or strand)

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_ids_of_gene_name
documentation:
What are the gene IDs associated with a given gene name?
(due to copy events, there might be multiple genes per name)

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.gene_id_of_protein_id
documentation: What is the gene ID associated with a given protein ID?

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.transcripts
documentation:
Construct Transcript object for every transcript entry in
the database. Optionally restrict to a particular
chromosome using the `contig` argument.
Transcript Info Objects

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.transcript_by_id
documentation: Construct Transcript object with given transcript ID

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._query_transcript_names
documentation: Transcript Names

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.transcript_names
documentation:
What are all the transcript names in the database
(optionally, restrict to a given chromosome and/or strand)

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._query_transcript_ids
documentation: Transcript IDs

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.transcript_id_of_protein_id
documentation: What is the transcript ID associated with a given protein ID?

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.exons
documentation:
Create exon object for all exons in the database, optionally
restrict to a particular chromosome using the `contig` argument.
Exon Info Objects

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.exon_by_id
documentation:
Construct an Exon object from its ID by looking up the exon"s
properties in the given Database.

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome._query_exon_ids
documentation: Exon IDs

methods.name: pyensembl.genome.Genome.protein_ids
documentation:
What are all the protein IDs
(optionally restrict to a given chromosome and/or strand)
Protein IDs

Bundles together the genomic annotation and sequence data associated with
a particular genomic database source (e.g. a single Ensembl release) and
provides a wide variety of helper methods for accessing this data.

modules.name: pyensembl.shell
documentation:
Manipulate pyensembl's local cache.

    %(prog)s {install, delete, delete-sequence-cache} [--release XXX --species human...]

To install particular Ensembl human release(s):
    %(prog)s install --release 75 77

To install particular Ensembl mouse release(s):
    %(prog)s install --release 75 77 --species mouse

To delete all downloaded and cached data for a particular Ensembl release:
    %(prog)s delete-all-files --release 75 --species human

To delete only cached data related to transcript and protein sequences:
    %(prog)s delete-index-files --release 75

To list all installed genomes:
    %(prog)s list

To install a genome from source files:
    %(prog)s install  --reference-name "GRCh38"  --gtf URL_OR_PATH  --transcript-fasta URL_OR_PATH  --protein-fasta URL_OR_PATH
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: pyensembl.shell.all_combinations_of_ensembl_genomes
documentation:
Use all combinations of species and release versions specified by the
commandline arguments to return a list of EnsemblRelease or Genome objects.
The results will typically be of type EnsemblRelease unless the
--custom-mirror argument was given.

functions.name: pyensembl.common._memoize_cache_key
documentation:
Turn args tuple and kwargs dictionary into a hashable key.

Expects that all arguments to a memoized function are either hashable
or can be uniquely identified from type(arg) and repr(arg).

functions.name: pyensembl.common.memoize
documentation:
Simple reset-able memoization decorator for functions and methods,
assumes that all arguments to the function can be hashed and
compared.

modules.name: pyensembl.ensembl_url_templates
documentation:
Templates for URLs and paths to specific relase, species, and file type
on the Ensembl ftp server.

For example, the human chromosomal DNA sequences for release 78 are in:

    https://ftp.ensembl.org/pub/release-78/fasta/homo_sapiens/dna/
Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at
http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

functions.name: pyensembl.ensembl_url_templates.normalize_release_properties
documentation:
Make sure a given release is valid, normalize it to be an integer,
normalize the species name, and get its associated reference.

functions.name: pyensembl.ensembl_url_templates.make_gtf_filename
documentation:
Return GTF filename expect on Ensembl FTP server for a specific
species/release combination

functions.name: pyensembl.ensembl_url_templates.make_gtf_url
documentation: Returns a URL and a filename, which can be joined together.

functions.name: pyensembl.ensembl_url_templates.make_fasta_url
documentation:
Construct URL to FASTA file with cDNA transcript or protein sequences

Parameter examples:
    ensembl_release = 75
    species = "Homo_sapiens"
    sequence_type = "cdna" (other option: "pep")

functions.name: pyensembl.reference_name.normalize_reference_name
documentation:
Search the dictionary of species-specific references to find a reference
name that matches aside from capitalization.

If no matching reference is found, raise an exception.

functions.name: pyensembl.reference_name.genome_for_reference_name
documentation:
Given a genome reference name, such as "GRCh38", returns the
corresponding Ensembl Release object.

If `allow_older_downloaded_release` is True, and some older releases have
been downloaded, then return the most recent locally available release.

Otherwise, return the newest release of Ensembl (even if its data hasn't
already been downloaded).

classes.name: pyensembl.database.Database

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
gtf_path : str
    Path to GTF annotation file

install_string : str
    Message to tell user if database connection is requested before
    database is created.

cache_directory_path : str
    Path to directory where database should be written. If omitted
    then use path of GTF file.

restrict_gtf_columns : list/set of str or None
    If provided then extract only these columns before creating
    a database.

restrict_gtf_features : list/set of str or None
    If provided then only create tables for these features.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database._all_possible_indices
documentation:
Create list of tuples containing all possible index groups
we might want to create over tables in this database.

If a set of genome annotations is missing some column we want
to index on, we have to drop any indices which use that column.

A specific table may later drop some of these indices if they're
missing  values for that feature or are the same as the table's primary key.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database._get_primary_key
documentation:
Name of primary key for a feature table (e.g. "gene" -> "gene_id")

Since we're potentially going to run this code over unseen data,
make sure that the primary is unique and never null.

If a feature doesn't have a primary key, return None.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database._feature_indices
documentation:
Choose subset of index group tuples from `all_index_groups` which are
applicable to a particular feature (not same as its primary key, have
non-null values).

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.create
documentation:
Create the local database (including indexing) if it's not
already set up. If `overwrite` is True, always re-create
the database from scratch.

Returns a connection to the database.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.connection
documentation: Get a connection to the database or raise an exception

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.connect_or_create
documentation:
Return a connection to the database if it exists, otherwise create it.
Overwrite the existing database if `overwrite` is True.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.column_values_at_locus
documentation:
Get the non-null values of a column from the database
at a particular range of loci

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.distinct_column_values_at_locus
documentation:
Gather all the distinct values for a property/column at some specified
locus.

Parameters
----------
column : str
    Which property are we getting the values of.

feature : str
    Which type of entry (e.g. transcript, exon, gene) is the property
    associated with?

contig : str
    Chromosome or unplaced contig name

position : int
    Chromosomal position

end : int, optional
    End position of a range, if unspecified assume we're only looking
    at the single given position.

strand : str, optional
    Either the positive ('+') or negative strand ('-'). If unspecified
    then check for values on either strand.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.run_sql_query
documentation:
Given an arbitrary SQL query, run it against the database
and return the results.

Parameters
----------
sql : str
    SQL query

required : bool
    Raise an error if no results found in the database

query_params : list
    For each '?' in the query there must be a corresponding value in
    this list.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.query
documentation:
Construct a SQL query and run against the sqlite3 database,
filtered both by the feature type and a user-provided column/value.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.query_feature_values
documentation:
Run a SQL query against the sqlite3 database, filtered
only on the feature type.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.query_loci
documentation:
Query for loci satisfying a given filter and feature type.


Parameters
----------
filter_column : str
    Name of column to filter results by.

filter_value : str
    Only return loci which have this value in the their filter_column.

feature : str
    Feature names such as 'transcript', 'gene', and 'exon'

Returns list of Locus objects

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database.query_locus
documentation:
Query for unique locus, raises error if missing or more than
one locus in the database.

Parameters
----------
filter_column : str
    Name of column to filter results by.

filter_value : str
    Only return loci which have this value in the their filter_column.

feature : str
    Feature names such as 'transcript', 'gene', and 'exon'

Returns single Locus object.

methods.name: pyensembl.database.Database._load_gtf_as_dataframe
documentation: Parse this genome source's GTF file and load it as a Pandas DataFrame

Wrapper around sqlite3 database so that the rest of the
library doesn't have to worry about constructing the .db file or
writing SQL queries directly.

modules.name: pyensembl.fasta
documentation:
The worse sin in bioinformatics is to write your own FASTA parser.
Unfortunately, small errors creep in to different FASTA files on the
Ensembl FTP server that no proper FASTA parser lets you skip over.
Copyright (c) 2015-2016. Mount Sinai School of Medicine

functions.name: pyensembl.fasta._parse_header_id
documentation:
Pull the transcript or protein identifier from the header line
which starts with '>'

functions.name: pyensembl.fasta.parse_fasta_dictionary
documentation:
Given a path to a FASTA (or compressed FASTA) file, returns a dictionary
mapping its sequence identifiers to sequences.

Parameters
----------
fasta_path : str
    Path to the FASTA file.

Returns dictionary from string identifiers to string sequences.

classes.name: pyensembl.fasta.FastaParser

methods.name: pyensembl.fasta.FastaParser.read_file
documentation: Read the contents of a FASTA file into a dictionary

methods.name: pyensembl.fasta.FastaParser.iterate_over_file
documentation: Generator that yields identifiers paired with sequences.

methods.name: pyensembl.fasta.FastaParser._open
documentation: Open either a text file or compressed gzip file as a stream of bytes.

FastaParser object consumes lines of a FASTA file incrementally
while building up a dictionary mapping sequence identifiers to sequences.

classes.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.__init__
documentation:
contig : str
    Chromosome or other sequence name in the reference assembly

start : int
    Start position of locus on the contig

end : int
    Inclusive end position on the contig

strand : str
    Should we read the locus forwards ('+') or backwards ('-')?

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.offset
documentation:
Offset of given position from stranded start of this locus.

For example, if a Locus goes from 10..20 and is on the negative strand,
then the offset of position 13 is 7, whereas if the Locus is on the
positive strand, then the offset is 3.

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.offset_range
documentation:
Database start/end entries are always ordered such that
start < end. This makes computing a relative position (e.g. of a stop
codon relative to its transcript) complicated since the "end"
position of a backwards locus is actually earlir on the strand.
This function correctly selects a start vs. end value depending
on this locuses's strand and determines that position's offset from
the earliest position in this locus.

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.can_overlap
documentation: Is this locus on the same contig and (optionally) on the same strand?

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.distance_to_interval
documentation:
Find the distance between intervals [start1, end1] and [start2, end2].
If the intervals overlap then the distance is 0.

methods.name: pyensembl.locus.Locus.overlaps
documentation:
Does this locus overlap with a given range of positions?

Since locus position ranges are inclusive, we should make sure
that e.g. chr1:10-10 overlaps with chr1:10-10

Base class for any entity which can be localized at a range of positions
on a particular strand of a chromosome/contig.

functions.name: pyensembl.download_cache.cache_subdirectory
documentation:
Which cache subdirectory to use for a given annotation database
over a particular reference. All arguments can be omitted to just get
the base subdirectory for all pyensembl cached datasets.

classes.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
reference_name : str
    Name of reference genome

annotation_name : str
    Name of annotation database

annotation_version : str or int, optional
    Version or release of annotation database

decompress_on_download : bool, optional
    If downloading a .fa.gz file, should we automatically expand it
    into a decompressed FASTA file?

copy_local_files_to_cache : bool, optional
    If file is on the local file system, should we still copy it
    into the cache?

install_string_function : fn, optional
    Function which returns an error message with
    install instructions. If not provided then the error tells the
    user what data is missing without install instructions.

cache_directory_path : str, optional
    Where to place downloaded and temporary files, by default
    inferred from reference name, annotation name, annotation version,
    and the global cache directory determined by datacache.

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache._fields
documentation: Fields used for hashing, string representation, equality comparison

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache.is_url_format
documentation:
Is the given string a URL?

Parameters
----------
path_or_url : str

Returns
-------
bool

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache._remove_compression_suffix_if_present
documentation:
If the given filename ends in one of the compression suffixes that
datacache knows how to deal with, remove the suffix (since we expect
the result of downloading to be a decompressed file)

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache.cached_path
documentation:
When downloading remote files, the default behavior is to name local
files the same as their remote counterparts.

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache._download_if_necessary
documentation: Return local cached path to a remote file, download it if necessary.

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache._copy_if_necessary
documentation: Return cached path to local file, copying it to the cache if necessary.

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache.download_or_copy_if_necessary
documentation:
Download a remote file or copy
Get the local path to a possibly remote file.

Download if file is missing from the cache directory and
`download_if_missing` is True. Download even if local file exists if
both `download_if_missing` and `overwrite` are True.

If the file is on the local file system then return its path, unless
self.copy_local_to_cache is True, and then copy it to the cache first.

Parameters
----------
path_or_url : str

download_if_missing : bool, optional
    Download files if missing from local cache

overwrite : bool, optional
    Overwrite existing copy if it exists

methods.name: pyensembl.download_cache.DownloadCache.delete_cached_files
documentation: Deletes any cached files matching the prefixes or suffixes given

Downloads remote files to cache, optionally copies local files into cache,
raises custom message if data is missing.

functions.name: pyensembl.ensembl_release.cached_release
documentation:
Create an EnsemblRelease instance only if it's hasn't already been made,
otherwise returns the old instance.
Keeping this function for backwards compatibility but this functionality
has been moving into the cached method of EnsemblRelease.

classes.name: pyensembl.ensembl_release.EnsemblRelease

methods.name: pyensembl.ensembl_release.EnsemblRelease.normalize_init_values
documentation:
Normalizes the arguments which uniquely specify an EnsemblRelease
genome.

methods.name: pyensembl.ensembl_release.EnsemblRelease.cached
documentation:
Construct EnsemblRelease if it's never been made before, otherwise
return an old instance.

methods.name: pyensembl.ensembl_release.EnsemblRelease.from_dict
documentation: Deserialize EnsemblRelease without creating duplicate instances.

Bundles together the genomic annotation and sequence data associated with
a particular release of the Ensembl database.

functions.name: pyensembl.species.normalize_species_name
documentation:
If species name was "Homo sapiens" then replace spaces with underscores
and return "homo_sapiens". Also replace common names like "human" with
"homo_sapiens".

functions.name: pyensembl.species.check_species_object
documentation: Helper for validating user supplied species names or objects.

classes.name: pyensembl.species.Species

methods.name: pyensembl.species.Species.register
documentation:
Create a Species object from the given arguments and enter into
all the dicts used to look the species up by its fields.

methods.name: pyensembl.species.Species.all_registered_latin_names
documentation: Returns latin name of every registered species.

methods.name: pyensembl.species.Species.all_species_release_pairs
documentation:
Generator which yields (species, release) pairs
for all possible combinations.

methods.name: pyensembl.species.Species.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
latin_name : str

synonyms : list of strings

reference_assemblies : dict
    Mapping of names of reference genomes onto inclusive ranges of
    Ensembl releases Example: {"GRCh37": (54, 75)}

Container for combined information about a species name, its synonyn names
and which reference to use for this species in each Ensembl release.
